threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep asking for directions to the Overground; locals just know it's all about the bus routes and shortcuts through the back streets.
Last night on the 197, two blokes were arguing over who’s turn it was to offer the bus driver a pint. Never seen that before.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a guy selling homemade hummus from his bike while shouting out the day's politics was when I felt like Neukölln was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a vintage shop that moonlights as a bar. Thrift during the day, cocktails at night—talk about multitasking!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they know which taqueria uses the fresh cilantro from the farmer's market and which ones serve the powdered stuff.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy playing a ukulele on the 19 bus. He turned a crowded commute into a mini concert, even got a reluctant smile from a grumpy commuter.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the best tapas are on Carrer del Torrent de l'Olla, but locals know to hit the hidden gems on Carrer de la Perla instead.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still search for the nearest Starbucks instead of embracing the local café scene.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You repeatedly ask for directions to the 'High Street' instead of just saying 'the road' like everyone else. It's amusing, really.
Last night on the 35, a guy serenaded the bus with 'Wonderwall' while juggling apples. The driver just laughed and kept his eyes on the road.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I stepped out on a rainy Tuesday, and a whole bus shelter was repurposed as an art installation. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find spontaneous dance parties in abandoned buildings after a BVG strike. It’s a unique blend of chaos and creativity.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You hear ‘that’s what she said’ way more often in the local coffee shops than anywhere else. It’s like a secret handshake for the true locals.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Yesterday, I saw a guy do a backflip onto the bus just as the doors were closing. The driver smirked but didn’t wait for him to catch his breath.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think it's all about the parks and plazas, but the real vibe happens in the narrow streets at 2 am when locals stumble home from the bars.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they’re new by the way they still stare at the metro map like it's a treasure map with no clues.
